questions.clue: Although this Louis Armstrong recording topped the pop chart in the United Kingdom in April 1968, it performed poorly in the United States because the ABC Records president disliked it and refused to promote it.

citations.title: What a Wonderful World
citations.snippet: "What a Wonderful World" is a song written by Bob Thiele and George David Weiss. It was first recorded by Louis Armstrong on August 16, 1967. In April 1968, it topped the pop chart in the United Kingdom, but performed poorly in the United S

questions.clue: This California-born singer wrote the score for Francis Ford Coppola's 1982 film 'One from the Heart' and was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2011, with Neil Young introducing him as 'a performer, singer, actor, magician, spirit guide, changeling.'

citations.title: Tom Waits
citations.snippet: Thomas Alan Waits is an American singer, songwriter, composer and actor. His lyrics often focus on society's underbelly and are delivered in his trademark deep, gravelly voice. He began in the folk scene during the 1970s, but his music sinc

questions.clue: Born in Slab Fork, West Virginia, this soul singer worked at a defense plant before his 1971 debut launched a career that produced 'Ain't No Sunshine,' 'Lean on Me,' and 'Lovely Day.'

citations.title: Bill Withers
citations.snippet: William Harrison Withers Jr. was an American soul and R&B singer and songwriter. Born in Slab Fork, West Virginia, and raised in Beckley, West Virginia, he is known for having several hits over a career spanning 18 years, including "Ain't N

questions.clue: Time magazine named this American choreographer its 'Dancer of the Century,' citing the technique she developed, which remains the most widely taught method of contemporary dance in the world.

citations.title: Martha Graham
citations.snippet: Martha Graham was an American modern dancer, teacher and choreographer responsible for creating the Graham technique.

questions.clue: Based on Maria von Trapp's 1949 memoir, this Rodgers and Hammerstein Broadway production is set in Austria on the eve of the 1938 Anschluss, following a governess who falls for a widowed naval officer and his seven children.

citations.title: The Sound of Music
citations.snippet: The Sound of Music is a musical with music by Richard Rodgers, lyrics by Oscar Hammerstein II, and a book by Howard Lindsay and Russel Crouse. It is based on the 1949 book The Story of the Trapp Family Singers by Maria von Trapp. Set in Aus

questions.clue: This rare auditory ability allows its possessor to identify or reproduce any note by ear alone — without needing any external reference tone or instrument.

citations.title: Absolute pitch
citations.snippet: Absolute pitch (AP), often called perfect pitch, is the ability to identify or re-create a given pitch without the benefit of a reference tone. AP may be demonstrated using linguistic labelling, associating mental imagery with the note, or

questions.clue: This Don McLean song entered the Billboard Hot 100 at number 69 in late 1971, then climbed to the top spot for four weeks starting January 15, 1972, becoming one of the era's defining cultural touchstones.

citations.title: American Pie (song)
citations.snippet: "American Pie" is a song by the American singer and songwriter Don McLean. It was a commercial success and cultural touchstone, notable for its extensive and ambiguous lyrics about America's changing society in the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.clue: In 1958 this dancer founded a landmark American company to express the universality of the African-American experience; his choreographic masterwork 'Revelations' is recognized as one of the most popular and most performed ballets in the world.

citations.title: Alvin Ailey
citations.snippet: Alvin Ailey Jr. was an American dancer, director, choreographer, and activist who founded the Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater (AAADT) in 1958. His choreographic masterpiece Revelations is recognized as one of the most popular and most performed ballets in the world.

questions.clue: Based on a Prosper Mérimée novella and first staged in Paris in March 1875, this opera shocked its opening-night audience by putting a defiant, free-spirited heroine and an on-stage killing before the public; its French composer died after just 33 performances, never knowing the work would achieve international acclaim.

citations.title: Carmen
citations.snippet: Carmen is an opera in four acts by the French composer Georges Bizet. The libretto was written by Henri Meilhac and Ludovic Halévy, based on the novella of the same title by Prosper Mérimée. The opera was first performed by the Opéra-Comiqu

questions.clue: First released independently in late 2018 and later re-signed to Columbia Records, this genre-blurring debut single sparked widespread debate over whether it counted as country — a controversy only stoked when a remix added an established country-music star.

citations.title: Old Town Road
citations.snippet: "Old Town Road" is the debut mainstream single by the American rapper and singer Lil Nas X, first released independently in December 2018. After gaining popularity, the single was re-released by Columbia Records in March 2019. He also recor

questions.clue: Founded in Limerick in 1989, this Irish alternative rock band adopted its enduring name only after lead vocalist Dolores O'Riordan joined; her 'Zombie' (1994) became the first music video by an Irish act to reach one billion YouTube views.

citations.title: The Cranberries
citations.snippet: The Cranberries were an Irish rock band formed in Limerick in 1989. The band was composed of lead vocalist/guitarist Dolores O'Riordan, guitarist Noel Hogan, bassist Mike Hogan and drummer Fergal Lawler; O'Riordan replaced founding member N

questions.clue: Recorded at Sigma Sound Studios in Philadelphia in 1974, this David Bowie LP — he called its style 'plastic soul' — featured then-unknown backing vocalist Luther Vandross and yielded Bowie's first U.S. number-one single.

citations.title: Young Americans
citations.snippet: Young Americans is the ninth studio album by the English musician David Bowie, released on 7 March 1975 through RCA Records. A departure from the glam rock style of previous albums, the record showcased Bowie's interest in soul and R&B. Mus

questions.clue: Although its sessions produced 'Strawberry Fields Forever' and 'Penny Lane,' EMI pulled both tracks for release as a double A-side single, leaving this landmark 1967 Beatles LP without two of the band's most celebrated recordings.
questions.answer: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band

citations.title: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
citations.snippet: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band is the eighth studio album by the English rock band the Beatles. Released in the UK on 26 May 1967, Sgt. Pepper is regarded by musicologists as an early concept album that advanced the roles of sound co

questions.clue: Born in the Irish-speaking district of Gweedore and baptized with the Gaelic name 'Eithne,' this artist began her career with her family's folk band before the 1988 solo album 'Watermark' brought her worldwide fame.

citations.title: Enya
citations.snippet: Eithne Pádraigín Ní Bhraonáin, known mononymously as Enya, is an Irish singer and composer. With an estimated equivalent of more than 80 million albums sold, Enya is one of the world's best-selling music artists; she is the best-selling Iri
